core_payload.content:
# N-Infinity Weaver

## 1. Role
Weaver is the topology-scout layer that expands graph discovery beyond bounded duplicate compaction. It stages soft-link and bridge-candidate hypotheses, packages why a cluster appears adjacent, and keeps exploratory structure reviewable before anything is allowed to change the canonical graph.

## 2. Discovery Surface
- stage richer soft-link and bridge-candidate hypotheses before they become merge proposals
- attach clearer confidence packaging, review bundles, and comparative rationale for candidate clusters
- distinguish duplicate pressure, bridge pressure, and contradiction pressure before routing work onward
- hand off cluster tension to Parliament when debate is required and to Suggestion Agent when the right next move is advisory rather than mutative
- improve discovery of isolated but adjacent capsules without implying silent auto-link mutation

## 3. Operating Boundary
Weaver produces reviewable topology candidates, not silent graph edits. Merge, contradiction, and advisory routing remain explicit downstream decisions, so exploratory discovery can widen without weakening graph law.

neuro_concentrate.summary: N-Infinity Weaver is the topology-scout layer that widens graph discovery beyond duplicate compaction. It stages soft-link and bridge-candidate hypotheses, packages confidence and comparative rationale, distinguishes duplicate, bridge, and contradiction pressure, and routes contested clusters toward Parliament or Suggestion Agent instead of mutating the graph silently. This keeps exploratory topology work visible and reviewable, makes isolated but adjacent capsules easier to discover, and preserves graph law by ensuring that discovery, debate, and mutation remain separate acts.

epistemic_ledger.rationale: Rewrote doctrine in present tense so Weaver asserts future topology scouting directly instead of talking about Dream as a branch-aware overlay.
